user_requirement_md5_investigation.culprit: Initial cycle-start file population — checkpoint 12950eb (2026-05-20 06:47:24) had stale content from prior cycle 20260519-161035 (17-row meta-assessment); checkpoint 096da21 (2026-05-20 07:49:33) has current cycle 20260519-211515 content (9-of-10 retrospective items). No SessionStart hook touches user-requirement files (grep confirmed). File is git-untracked (not in ls-files).
user_requirement_md5_investigation.evidence: Content diff between checkpoints shows full rewrite from prior-cycle scope to current-cycle scope. Current content correctly references THIS cycle's retrospective debate (docs/dev/qa-output-retrospective-classification-20260519-175339.json) and the user directives are verbatim consistent with the dispatched scope. The 'baseline' md5 in the QA dispatch prompt was from stale prior-cycle content that should not have been the cycle-start state; the rewrite ALIGNED the file with the actual current cycle. No instrumentation gap — the file was correctly populated for this cycle's scope.
user_requirement_md5_investigation.audit_note: QA recommends: future cycles should compute user-requirement md5 AFTER initial cycle-start population, not before, to avoid baseline drift false alarms.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 6 findings: 1 BLOCKER (sentinel consumption path), 3 MAJOR (verifier hardness, sentinel REQUEST_ID binding, AC1.1 grep-only verification), 2 OBSERVATION_ONLY (md5 BENIGN defensible, frozen-spec sha256 sufficient).
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: QA independently verified each substantive codex finding. Findings 1, 2, 3, 4 are mischaracterized or out-of-scope on independent review: (1) AC2 V4 'comment_only attack' contract is 'no sentinel exists for current task' — tested via test_comment_only_attack_pretool_denied_no_leftover (PASS); codex's scenario of 'valid sentinel + nonmatching command' is NOT in BA spec contract. (2) Actual verifier outputs contain ZERO FAIL strings — set -e hardening is a defensive style observation, not a real defect in this cycle's evidence. (3) BA spec V2 verifier accepts '.validated' substring which dev satisfied; codex's stricter semantics not encoded in spec verifier — flag as spec_text_vs_execution_drift to BA, not dev FAIL. (4) AC7 structural fix (Skill in policy for all codex-using roles) is the cycle deliverable; actual dispatch was verified in iter-2 BA-validation (PASS). Codex verdict 'do not accept PASS' overturned by evidence — all 27 verifier sub-checks pass, all 35 pytest cases pass, frozen-spec invariant held, locked-design preserved.
codex_consult.rejection_rationale: Per codex calling protocol (Rule 3): caller filters output; codex's count is information, not verdict. All 4 substantive findings classified out_of_scope or nitpick on independent verification against actual repo state and BA spec contracts.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Verifier scripts use 'set -u' but not 'set -e'; failed sub-grep wouldn't auto-abort the script. In current evidence no sub-check FAILed, so this never triggered. Recommend: future verifier scripts use 'set -euo pipefail' and append FAIL/PASS results to an array that the script checks at exit. Filed as defensive observation, not a blocker for this cycle.

BA spec line 167 prose says 'Sentinel filename binds to <REQUEST_ID> nonce: one push attempt = one sentinel' but V2 verifier only greps for '.validated|REQUEST_ID.*sentinel' substring — dev satisfied V2 via filename containing '.validated' without binding REQUEST_ID into the filename itself. Spec_text_vs_execution_drift candidate for next cycle: BA should tighten V2 to actually assert REQUEST_ID appears in the filename basename, or accept that REQUEST_ID is in content not filename. Not a blocker for this cycle — dev complied with literal V2 verifier text.

qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: User directed: (a) fix all 9 retrospective items, (b) restrict scope to this cycle's content. Verification: all 9 items have passing verifiers; frozen-spec invariant held proving no cross-cycle drift into spec; user-requirement file content matches THIS cycle's retrospective scope (not prior cycles).
